cmes.note: This CME is initially bright but becomes faint and diffuse after a few frames in SOHO LASCO C2. The potential source for this CME is moving/opening field lines visible along/beyond the limb to the NW in GOES SUVI 284 starting around 2026-05-17T01:30Z. There are multiple overlapping fronts to the W with start times between 2026-05-17T00:36Z and 2026-05-17T10:24Z which include this CME in the mix.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky parameters based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2 frames before becoming too diffuse to track.

cmes.note: Faint CME visible to the W/NW in SOHO LASCO C2 and possibly in SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. There are multiple overlapping events to the W with start times between 2026-05-17T00:36Z and 2026-05-17T10:24Z which are difficult to separate outside of SOHO LASCO C2 frames. The source of this CME appears to be an M1.4 class flare from Active Region 14435 (N22W97) based on SDO/AIA 131/304, GOES SUVI 304/284, and STEREO A EUVI 304/195. The associated filament ejecta visible in SDO/AIA, GOES SUVI, and STEREO A EUVI 304 appears to initially deflect southward before returning to the solar disk instead of escaping the disk during the eruption.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2 imagery using the potential source location for longitude. There is some uncertainty with this measurement due to its faintness and diffuse leading edge.

cmes.note: This narrow CME is visible to the W in SOHO LASCO C2 and possibly in SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. There are multiple overlapping events to the W with start times between 2026-05-17T00:36Z and 2026-05-17T10:24Z. There is no clear source for this CME, but it may be associated with activity from AR 14435 or 14432 which are located beyond the W/NW limb.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement based on the best fit in SOHO LASCO C2.

cmes.note: Narrow, jet-like CME visible to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. There is no clear source for this CME.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: Minor narrow CME seen to the southeast in SOHO LASCO C2, not in C3 due to pylon obstruction, and to the southeast in GOES CCOR-1, seen to the southwest as a much wider CME in STEREO A COR2A. Based on the geometry and lack of any sources seen in EUV imagery, this CME appears to be far-sided relative to the Earth-Sun line.

cmeAnalyses.note: Leading edge measurement using GOES, STEREO Ahead, and SOHO spacecraft for triangulation of the longitude.

cmes.note: Faint, narrow, jet-like CME visible to the NE in STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery. There is no clear source for this CME.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: Faint CME visible to the W/NW in STEREO A COR2, SOHO LASCO C2/C3, and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. The potential source is an eruption from an AR on/beyond the W/NW limb in STEREO A EUVI 195/304 imagery seen as post-eruptive arcades and moving/opening field lines (195) and brightening (304) starting around 2026-05-18T11:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: Faint narrowish CME seen to the W in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 coronagraphs. No source has been found for this CME in EUV imagery, so it is likely far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2). No source has been found for it in EUV imagery.

cmes.note: Faint but fast CME with a dimpled, unclear front seen to the E in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 coronagraphs but fully covered by the night-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. The source of it is clearly far-sided, as indicated by a minor but clear movement/opening of field lines seen behind the E limb only in GOES SUVI 195 starting at 2026-05-18T21:05Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: The CME is fully covered by the night-time data gap in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph and its far-sided source is behind the east limb, as indicated by the opening of field lines seen in GOES SUVI 195. Only plane-of-sky analysis can be done.

cmes.note: CME associated with minor B8-class solar flare and point source eruption in Active Region 14440 (N17E37), seen to the northeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and to the east-northeast in STEREO A COR2A where it appears much narrower. The EUV signature is characterized by a point source brightening and spray seen best in SDO AIA 193, 171, and 304 starting around 2026-05-19T10:30Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Leading edge measurement not incorporating the narrow nose which accelerates seen in STEREO A COR2A, so the actual CME speed may be faster than this. Appears wider in SOHO LASCO, so an upper bound on width.

cmes.note: A faint minor CME seen to the NW in difference imagery of SOHO LASCO C2, also seen in C3 and GOES CCOR-1 and not seen in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph.  No clear source has been found for this CME in EUV imagery. There are follow-up, similar faint outflows following this CME.

cmeAnalyses.note: This faint CME is not seen in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph and no clear source for it has been found in EUV imagery, so only plane-of-sky analysis can be made.

cmes.note: Narrow CME seen to the east in SOHO LASCO C2 and SOHO LASCO C3 and to the east in GOES CCOR-1, not well seen in STEREO A COR2A, probably due to geometry (likely directed behind of the STEREO A-Sun line). Appears to be associated with eruptive activity beyond the eastern limb of the Earth facing disk as evidenced by moving/opening field lines seen best in GOES SUVI 284 around 2026-05-20T04:40Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Two spacecraft plane of sky measurement, since the source longitude is relatively uncertain. True half-width is less than 10 degrees.

cmes.note: CME seen to the south in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, and GOES CCOR-1 and to the southwest in STEREO A COR2A, likely associated with a far-sided eruption as suggested from geometry and lack of Earth-facing eruptive activity in EUV imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Leading edge measurement to triangulate the longitude.

cmes.note: Very minor, faint CME seen to the north in SOHO LASCO C2 and to the northeast in STEREO A COR2A; the source is a minor loop which is expelled at very high latitudes seen best in SDO AIA 171 around 2026-05-20T09:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Leading edge measurement which determined that the CME was out of the ecliptic plane.

cmes.note: Narrow faint CME seen to the E in GOES CCOR-1 coronagraph and not seen in STEREO A COR2.  At the moment it is covered by a large scheduled data gap in SOHO LASCO. Its start is likely earlier, which can be confirmed if LASCO backfills. Its source is not clear, and is likely far-sided, as possibly indicated by a minor movement of field lines behind the E limb seen in SDO AIA 193 after 14Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: The CME is not seen in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph and its source is not clear (it is likely far-sided). This plane-of-sky analysis is made at the time no LASCO imagery is available, with the use of only GOES CCOR-1 imagery.

cmes.note: Two-lobed CME with a somewhat irregular front seen to the NW in GOES CCOR-1 and a loop-like faint partial halo front seen to the NNE in COR2A and covered by the scheduled data gap in SOHO. The source is likely the eruption of a long filament east of AR 14436 (N20W55) best seen in STEREO A EUV 195/304 just E of the central meridian, as seen from STEREO A and in GOES SUVI 284 imagery close to the West limb.

cmeAnalyses.note: The CME was reanalyzed with later imagery and with inclusion of the more southern lobe of the CME in addition to its northern lobe. Analysis is based on the fit of the faint front in two coronagraphs (GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2) and takes into account the location of the erupting filament. LASCO imagery is absent at the time of analysis.

Very approximate analysis based on the fit of the larger lobe of this CME in early imagery in two coronagraphs, GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2, and roughly corresponding to the source eruption's parameters. The front of the CME is faint and somewhat unclear in both coronagraphs.

cmes.note: Minor faint CME seen to the E in GOES CCOR-1 coronagraph and not seen in STEREO A COR2. At this moment it is covered by a large scheduled data gap in SOHO LASCO. Its start is likely earlier, which can be confirmed if LASCO backfills. No source has been found for this CME.

cmeAnalyses.note: The CME is not seen in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph and its source is not clear. This plane-of-sky analysis is made at the time no LASCO imagery is available, with the use of only GOES CCOR-1 imagery.

cmes.note: Very faint narrow CME seen best to the SSW in GOES CCOR-1 and likely also seen to the SW in a few timestamps of STEREO A COR2, although very faintly. Its start is likely earlier and can be clarified if LASCO imagery backfills. The source is likely a prominence seen lifting off from behind the SW limb in STEREO A EUV 304 starting after 2026-05-20T16:45Z. This CME is followed by at least one outflow.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate and somewhat speculative analysis based on the fit of a very faint front in two coronagraphs, GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2, and loosely confirmed by a possible source located behind the SW limb as seen from STEREO A.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the northeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source for this event is unclear, but may be related to a minor eruption centered near N05E30 starting round 2026-05-21T07:00Z seen in SDO AIA 304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: CME visible to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. The source is an M2.3 flare peaking at 2026-05-22T10:29Z from AR 14436 (approximately N18W96) as seen in STEREO A EUV 195 imagery. Dimming is best seen in STEREO A EUV 195 imagery. Opening field lines and post-eruptive arcades are visible in SDO AIA 171/193/284 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Updated measurement based on source location as well as the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 in SWPC_CAT.
Preliminary measurement based on source location using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NW in GOES CCOR-1 starting around 2026-05-22T19:30Z. It is also seen to the NW in SOHO LASCO C3. It is missed in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 due to real-time data gaps. There is not a separate, front-sided source for this CME, so it is most likely far-sided and possibly outflow following earlier CME: 2026-05-22T10:48Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement chosen due to lack of available coronagraph triangulation and lack of visible source candidate.

cmes.note: Narrow CME with clear front in STEREO A COR2. The front in SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1 that possibly corresponds to this event is very faint and unclear. No clear source has been found for this CME, although there is a far-sided field line movement around 2026-05-23T02:11Z pretty clearly in SUVI 284, but the CME does not triangulate to the east limb.

cmeAnalyses.note: A very speculative analysis based on the fit with STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2/C3. While the COR2A front is bright, the front in LASCO is extremely faint. No source has been found on the earth-facing disk for this CME.

cmes.note: Faint loop CME to the E in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs. No clear source for this CME is seen in EUV imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Very approximate analysis based on the fit of the loop fronts in SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs and not confirmed by the source location as no source has been found in EUV imagery.

cmes.note: Narrow faint CME seen to the WNW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 and likely not seen in STEREO A COR2. No clear source for this CME has been found in EUV imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: It is not clear if the CME is seen in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph and no source has been found for it, so only plane-of-sky analysis can be done.

cmes.note: CME observed to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, and as a faint N/NW partial halo in STEREO A COR2. The source is very likely former AR 14436, located around N18 and W100-W110. Observed as field line movement and loops breaking over the NW limb in SDO AIA 171/193 starting around 2026-05-23T05:18Z, and directly observed in STEREO A EUVI 195 as slow dimming and brightening starting around 2026-05-23T06:05Z in a wide area around former AR 14436's location. This active region is in close proximity to a large coronal hole to its southwest, possibly affecting CME propagation.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between the clear view in LASCO C3 and faint, difficult to resolve view in STEREO A COR2. Despite the source being located around W105, this front does not fit well outside of the W70-W80 range, with available imagery and its quality. Attempts to fit around W105 do not match the COR2A structure, but no other corresponding eruptions or fronts to account for this are identified, so it is possible this CME is just greatly deflected away from the source coordinates.

A preliminary SOHO LASCO C3 analysis close to the approximate observed source of the CME around N18W105. This does not match the STEREO A COR2 front for full triangulation (see other CME analysis note), but is presented due to the magnitude of the other analysis' deflection.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the SE only in SOHO LASCO C2, better in running difference imagery, fading after a few early frames. The source is a rapid snap of field lines to the SE starting at 2026-05-23T07:19Z in GOES SUVI 284, from a likely far-sided source yet to rotate on.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2 only plane-of-sky.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the SE only in SOHO LASCO C2, better in running difference imagery, fading after a few early frames. The source is a rapid snap of field lines to the SE starting at 2026-05-23T11:51Z in GOES SUVI 284, from a likely far-sided source yet to rotate on.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2 only plane-of-sky.

cmes.note: CME observed to the NNE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, and due N in STEREO A COR2. Likely occulted by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1. Source is far-sided over the NE limb, observed best in GOES SUVI 284 as a wide breaking of field lines over the NE starting around 2026-05-23T14:43Z as part of a series of similar field line movements and CME fronts at this time.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 triangulation.

cmes.note: CME observed to the WNW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, and NW as a partial halo in STEREO A COR2. The source is just NW of AR 14441 (N08W67), centered around N18W75. First observed as field line movement and loop breaking on the NW limb in SDO AIA 171/193, and dimming in SDO AIA 193, starting around 2026-05-23T15:01Z; also observed well in STEREO A EUVI as a wide dimming Starting around 2026-05-23T16:05Z identifying AR 14441 as the source and not former AR 14436 located further behind the limb.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 triangulation.

cmes.note: CME observed to the NNE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, and due N in STEREO A COR2. Likely occulted by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1. Source is far-sided over the NE limb, observed best in GOES SUVI 284 as a wide breaking of field lines over the NE starting around 2026-05-23T16:15Z as part of a series of similar field line movements and CME fronts at this time.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 triangulation.

cmes.note: CME observed to the NNE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, and due N in STEREO A COR2. Likely occulted by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1. Source is far-sided over the NE limb, observed best in GOES SUVI 284 as a wide breaking of field lines over the NE starting around 2026-05-23T16:59Z as part of a series of similar field line movements and CME fronts at this time.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 triangulation.

cmes.note: Fast CME with a brighter, narrower bulk partially obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1 and a fainter halo shock seen to the N in COR2A and to the NE in CCOR-1 (and initially covered by the scheduled data gap in SOHO LASCO). It is associated with the fast far-sided eruption seen to the NNE in SOHO LASCO 171 and GOES SUVI 284 as fast movement of field lines and restructuring of magnetic field and as long, narrow strands of a filament in GOES SUVI 304. It is associated with type II radio burst.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate analysis of the brighter bulk based on the fit with two coronagraphs, GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.
Approximate analysis of the faint halo shock front based on the fit with two coronagraphs, GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.

Preliminary, very approximate analysis of the brighter bulk based on the fit of early coronagraph imagery with two coronagraphs, GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2. This far-sided CME is partially obscured by the pylon in CCOR-1 making analysis difficult.

cmes.note: CME seen to the NW in SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1 with an irregular front overlapping with earlier NW CMEs. The front is likely also seen to the NW in a couple of timestamps of STEREO A COR2. Its source is not clear: one source could be a minor eruption in AR 14441 (N8W68)seen at 2026-05-23T10:45 in STA EUV 304/195 seen as brightening and dimming but it is not confirmed by the fit with coronagraphs. Another source could be AR 14436 (N18W110) which has been showing signs of activity for many hours.

cmeAnalyses.note: Speculative, approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1) and on Active Region 14436 (N18W110) as a possible source.
